revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Atagoyama Kofun]][[ja:愛宕山古墳 (行田市)]]
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Gyōda, Japan|qq=}} {{About|the Kofun in Gyōda|the site in Mito|Atagoyama Kofun (Mito)|qq=}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Atagoyama Kofun | native_name =愛宕山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Atagoyama Kofun (Gyoda), funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Atagoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Saitama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Atagoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.126305555556|N|139.47697222222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Gyoda|qid=Q860659|1=Gyōda}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of ={{ill|Sakitama Kofun Cluster|qid=Q2213580|1=Sakitama Kofun Cluster}} | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 6th-7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]]|qq=}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Atagoyama Kofun'''|愛宕山古墳|||qq=}} is a ''[[kofun]]'' [[Kofun]] located in the city of [[:en:Gyōda, Saitama|Gyōda]], {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|en|Saitama Prefecture|qid=Q128186|lt=Saitama Prefecture|1=Saitama Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a National Historic Site in 1938 and re-designated as a [[Monuments of Japan|Special National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2020 as part of the [[Sakitama Kofun Cluster]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/603 |title=埼玉古墳群|trans-title=Saikitama Kofun Cluster|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 31, 2020|qq=}}</ref> ==Overview== The Atagoyama Kofun has a total length of 53 meters, and is the smallest tumulus in the Saitama Kofun Cluster. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|qq=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. As with the other keyhole-shaped tumuli in this cluster, the Atagoyama Kofun had a rectangular double moat. A large number of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been [[Archaeological excavation|excavated]] from this tumulus, including ''haniwa'' shaped as cylinders, "morning-glory", houses, horses, men with swords, and other male figures. The tumulus dates from the middleof the 6th century AD. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] has not been excavated as the mound was in very poor repair and was in danger of collapsing. Its name comes from an Atago Shrine which was once located on the tumulus. The Atagoyama Kofun is near the parking lot to the Sakitama Kofun archaeological park, and is adjacent to the [[Gyōda Futagoyama Kofun]], the largest ''kofun'' in the cluster. ;Overall length: 53 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 30 meter diameter x 3.4 meter high ;Anterior rectangular portion: 30 meters wide x 3.3 meters high ==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|qid=Q6573282|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Saitama)}} ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} ==External links== {{template-inline|Atagoyama Kofun (Gyoda)|qq=}} *[https://www.city.gyoda.lg.jp/mobile/15/04/12/map/atago.html Gyoda city home page] {{in lang|ja|qq=}} *[https://sakitama-muse.spec.ed.jp/埼玉古墳群紹介/愛宕山古墳 Museum of the Sakitama Ancient Kofuns] {{in lang|ja|qq=}}
{{Kofun navbar|qq=}}
